note: SNG-172. HOW a craft is POWERED — a separate axis from powerSystem, which is the ACCESS taxonomy (which reach granted it) and is load-bearing in progression.js. Never conflate them.
canonAmendment: RATIFIED by Erik 2026-07-19. The founding claim 'every craft in this world is nanite-mediated' is NO LONGER UNIVERSAL. Some power is natural — body, breath, growth, grief, craft-of-hand — and is not lattice-mediated at all.
ruling_natural_benefits: Erik 2026-07-19: natural craft BENEFITS from a thin lattice, not merely goes unpenalised. The Quickwood is not the Returned's tolerable ground — it is their BEST ground. This is what makes them equal rather than compensated.

sources.lattice: The Precursor lattice. Two-sided band: dense ground helps until it interferes.
sources.wild: Ungoverned nanites — the Wild Half's tangled current. Widens BOTH crit bands (SNG-140). Thrives in the unreached gaps (threeGrounds).
sources.natural: Not nanite-mediated. IMPROVED by thin lattice per Erik's ruling. Indifferent to interference from above.
sources.combination: Two sources at once, scaled by mix — never rounded to the dominant one.
sources.inherent: No machine at all. Direct contact with what the universe is doing anyway — endings, growth, feeling, stillness, meaning. The OLDEST way, and the one the lattice was built to replace. A dense lattice is INTERFERENCE: apparatus standing between the practitioner and the thing itself. Thin ground is a clear signal.
sources.material: Body, skill, stone, edge. Reaches for nothing and needs nothing, so nothing can be taken from it: a FLOOR, flat at every density. Erik's ruling — 'the pure never loses because it never leaned on anything.' Material AUGMENTED by another source is stronger than pure, but the augmentation carries the band, so in wrong ground an augmented craft degrades TOWARD its pure form rather than failing.

method: Classified against REGION SUBSTRATE DENSITY as evidence, with the craft's own description as the judgement. The prediction was recorded in SNG-172 §4 BEFORE the pass: 'most abilities in low-density traditions should come out natural or wild; if the pass produces a lattice-dominant Rootkin, the pass is wrong.' It held — see validation.

enginewright.why: Enginecraft IS the lattice running. Densest region in the world (0.98).

lattice.why: Latticework is built INTO the substrate; the city and the lattice are one structure (0.94).

seraphic.why: The Orders never abandoned the old frequencies — continuous with the pre-Transition world (0.88).

blazeborn.why: Radiance burns on lattice, not on fuel (0.86).

figurist.why: Formcraft works in pattern and idea — it needs a medium to impose them on (0.85).

hourkeeper.why: Duration is tended, not felt. Hourcraft reaches into the substrate to hold time still (0.84).

numinous.why: RECLASSIFIED 2026-07-19 (Erik). Was lattice 0.7 — I reasoned from their region's density and had it backwards. Nothing in Numenwork touches a machine: it senses where meaning runs dense, briefly makes a symbol real, strips weight from a belief. That is direct contact with no apparatus — inherent, and they are its origin. The residual lattice 0.25 is OLD HABIT, not dependence. Their dense home is their own accumulated work, not borrowed power.

churnfolk.why: Wildcraft is the named case. Constant productive disorder is what wild current DOES.

abyssal.why: The Descent communes with what the lattice became where nobody governed it (0.55).

unmaker.why: Ruinwork ends things well — it works with what is already coming apart (0.42).

veilwright.why: Falsecraft needs a medium to carry a false thing. Mid-high density (0.66) and it uses every bit.

umbral.why: Umbracraft sees by other means — partly instrument, partly trained sense (0.58).

cogitant.why: ERIK'S WORKED EXAMPLE: nanite to move matter with a mind, natural for the mind itself. Telekinesis borrows the lattice; the thought that aims it does not.

syllogist.why: Reason is natural; making a proof BIND is not (0.60).

horizon.why: Walking is natural. Folding the distance you walk is not (0.44).

wright.why: Makecraft is craft-of-hand that reaches for the lattice when the hand is not enough (0.52).

rootkin.why: Vivimancy GROWS. The Rootkin completed the Transition and released the lattice; the green current replaced it. Thinnest region in the world (0.12) and their best ground.

marcher.why: The Edge is skill at arms in a body. Nothing to borrow (0.20).

somatic.why: Soma is the perfected body. Somatic discipline is a retuning AWAY from the lattice (0.22).

mason.why: Thingcraft holds that only what can be touched is real — weight that stands without the lattice (0.30).

ashwarden.why: Palework tends endings. What tends endings does not hold a charge (0.32).

verist.why: Verity is speech and refusal. Saying what is so costs nothing but the saying (0.46).

threnodist.why: Pathos holds that feeling is the only true instrument. Feeling is not lattice-mediated (0.48).

stillhold.why: Stillcraft is a vow kept. The Stillhold gave the lattice up with the war.

perAbilityOverrides.note: Empty by design. A tradition-level default plus explicit deviations is far less content than 285 authored fields, and a deviation is the interesting fact. Add here only where an ability genuinely departs from its tradition's mix.
